---
description: "Analyze stash and backlog queue to identify contextually consistent groupings of work for staging and shipment"
agent: Stage
argument-hint: "[scope=priority-band-or-label]"
---

# Stage Grouping Analysis

Review the current items in the stash and the current items in the queue to determine which
items can be logically grouped together for staging and shipment. Items that are thematically
or contextually related, touch the same code surfaces, or depend on each other are candidates
for the same shipment. Grouping related work reduces shipment count and makes dependencies
easier to manage.

## Inputs

* `${input:scope}`: (Optional) Limit analysis to a specific priority band, label, or domain area. Defaults to all active stash entries and unshipped queued items.

## Requirements

1. Follow Steps 0, 1, and 1.5 of the Stage protocol.
2. Include both stash entries **and** queued items that are not yet assigned to an active or
   queued shipment. Eligible queue items can join a grouping with related stash entries so
   related work ships as one coherent unit rather than as separate shipments.
3. For each proposed grouping, note any queue items that would merge into the covering feature
   alongside the stash entries. Record the expected reduction in open item count.
4. Do not proceed to deliberation, planning, harvest, or shipment assembly until the operator
   confirms a grouping selection. Present grouping proposals and pause for operator input.
